questions.subject: NSEP
questions.q:
Consider the following physical expressions
(I) $\rho v^2(\rho$ : density, $v:$ velocity $)$
(II) $\frac{\mathrm{Y} \Delta \mathrm{L}}{\mathrm{L}}$ (Y: Young's modulus, L: length)
(III) $\frac{\sigma^2}{\varepsilon_0}$ ( $\sigma$ : surface density of charge)
(IV) $h \rho r$ ( $h$ : rise of a liquid in a capillary tube of radius $r$ )
The expressions having same dimensional formula are

questions.subject: NSEP
questions.q: Which of the following physical quantities have dimensions identical to each other?
options: $\epsilon_0 E^2$ where $E$ is the electric field intensity and $\epsilon_0$ is the permittivity of free space.
$\frac{B^2}{\mu_0}$ where $B$ is the magnetic field and $\mu_0$ is the permeability of free space.
$k T$ where $k$ is Boltzmann's constant and $T$ is the absolute temperature.
